title: astragalus lentiginosus var. semotus
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. semotus, also known as the Little Barren Medick, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which are believed to help the body adapt to stress and promote immune function. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Astragalus lentiginosus var. semotus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
